description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

On March 30 (Monday), the General Incorporated Association Saitama Truck Association donated 508 illuminated truck-shaped safety buzzers to Hanno City.

The Saitama Truck Association has made "promotion of social contribution activities" one of its priority initiatives to help create a safe and secure life for prefectural residents. In July 2005, they signed a "Crime Prevention Town Development Agreement" with Saitama Prefecture and the Saitama Police to protect children, women, elderly people and others from street crimes and similar incidents. As part of these efforts, since fiscal year 2006 they have been manufacturing illuminated truck-shaped safety buzzers and donating them to newly enrolled elementary school students. This time, they have donated 508 units to children entering Hanno City municipal elementary schools.

The illuminated truck-shaped safety buzzers donated this time will be distributed to first-grade students entering Hanno City municipal elementary schools in fiscal year 2026 (Reiwa 8). These will be used effectively to help ensure children's safety and deter crime against children.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/shiseijoho/shichoshitsu/shichoblog/shisei2026/shisei202603/13371.html
summary: 📝 Mayor: Saitama Truck Association donates 508 illuminated truck-shaped safety buzzers
